# Atlantia Lore

Atlantia is a small coastal town on the eastern edge of the known shore, built where rocky hills lean down toward a cold, changeable sea. It is large enough to have old families, public obligations, and quiet rivalries, but small enough that grief, scandal, and kindness all travel by name.

## Town Overview

On the edge of town a road climb inland toward Sunstone Mage College, another climbs the shore towards the watch tower. 

Fishing boats, supply wagons, traveling performers, and college messengers keep the town connected to the wider world, but Atlantia still thinks of itself as a place apart.

The town is practical before it is grand. Stone walls are patched instead of replaced, family homes are expanded one room at a time, and most public arguments eventually become private bargains. 
People remember who helped during storms and who looked away.

## The Harbor

The harbor is the town's busiest area. Nets dry along the lower railings, gulls gather near the fish stalls, and sailors trade news before they trade coin. On calm mornings the water looks almost silver, but locals know how quickly the coast can turn dangerous.

The sea is also where many Atlantian stories become uncertain. Ships leave with promises, return with missing crew, or fail to return at all. Some families prefer practical explanations. Others keep older, stranger stories alive because grief is sometimes easier to carry when it has a shape.

## The Watch Tower

The watch tower stands above the harbor road and remains one of Atlantia's oldest civic duties. It began as a warning post for storms, raiders, and shipwrecks, but over time it became a symbol of who the town trusts to keep watch while others sleep.

The Ravenmark family has long been associated with the tower. The work is less glamorous than outsiders imagine: long hours, salt-stung windows, signal lamps, weather logs, and the discipline to notice small changes on the horizon.

## Sunstone Mage College

Sunstone Mage College sits along the rocky outcrop beyond town, close enough to influence Atlantia and distant enough to feel like its own world. Students and teachers bring money, disputes, strange supplies, and stranger questions into town.

Atlantia depends on the college more than it always admits. The college buys food, hires local labor, commissions repairs, and occasionally asks the town to tolerate experiments best discussed after the fact. In return, the town offers the college a human scale: gossip, errands, debts, music, and weathered common sense.

## Surrounding Roads

The inland road climbs toward the college and then breaks into smaller routes through farms, scrub woods, and old stone markers. The coast road is harsher, but it carries travelers who prefer the sea air or need to avoid attention.

Between town and college, it is easy for people to become half-local: known by face, not always by history. That makes Atlantia a useful place for characters with complicated pasts. The town notices everything, but it does not always ask directly.

## Story Hooks

- A storm damages the watch tower lamp just before ships are expected home.
- A college errand goes missing between Sunstone and the harbor.
- Old tower logs contradict a family's accepted version of a death at sea.
- A traveling performer recognizes a name the town has tried to forget.
